articles.title: Iran sends tankers loaded with oil past US military blockade
articles.summary: Three Iranian tankers loaded with crude oil have passed the US blockade line in the Gulf of Oman, ship-tracking data shows.

articles.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
articles.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

articles.title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
articles.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

articles.title: Ex-Nigeria oil minister cleared in UK bribery trial
articles.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke had been accused of receiving bribes from oil tycoons but was found not guilty by a jury at Southwark Crown Court.

articles.title: Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life
articles.summary: In fiery exchange, judge sentenced him to three life sentences plus 25 years to life on four other charges, all running consecutively.

articles.title: Norway's crown princess undergoes successful lung transplant, palace says
articles.summary: Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffered from pulmonary fibrosis, will now spend several weeks recovering in hospital.

articles.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
articles.summary: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".

articles.title: Bystanders smash windows of private jet to rescue passengers after crash
articles.summary: One of the six people on board died in the crash, a local official said. The five survivors were taken to hospital and are in a stable condition.

articles.title: AI will create more jobs for humans, not replace them, Amazon founder Bezos says
articles.summary: The Amazon founder, who now has robotics and space travel companies, thinks AI will create a labour shortage.

articles.title: Group planned to attack White House UFC event using snipers and drones, FBI says
articles.summary: In newly unsealed court filings, the government says the group allegedly expressed grievances about corruption, the Epstein files, and data centres.
